channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: California State Assembly Passes 'Protect Our Games Act'
item.content:encoded:
In a victory for the 'Stop Killing Games' movement, the California State Assembly today passed Assembly Bill 1921, which is called the Protect Our Games Act. 

The Protect Our Games Act is one of a number of initiatives that have come from the 'Stop Killing Games' movement and is the furthest along as far as legislation goes. It passed with a vote of 43 to 16, largely along party lines with 41 democrats in favor (1 opposed), and 15 republicans opposed (2 in favor). A total of 21 assembly members were also absent from the vote.

The Protect Our Games Act requires that games released starting January 1st, 2027, provide at least 60 days notice before shutting down necessary support such as mandatory servers. They are also required to provide a version of the game that can be used without support from the developer or to provide full refunds to everyone who purchased the game. It also prohibits the sale of such unsupported games. 

The Protect Our Games Act doesn't apply in all cases. It notably doesn't apply to Free-to-play games, subscription-based games, or to games that were released or re-released before January 1st 2027, as well as titles where a permanent offline downloadable version is available.

One area of potential ambiguity is where the line of 'ordinary use' applies, and how much of a game can be taken offline before it contravines that. In the Act, ordinary use is defined as "a purchaser’s ability to use the core features of a digital game, consistent with the reasonable expectations of a purchaser based on how the digital game was advertised, marketed, or otherwise described by the digital game operator at the time of purchase", but many games have multiple modes and people may view different parts as core features.

For now, the Protect Our Games Act is off to the California State Senate where it will seek to pass, and then must also be signed into law by the California Governor. 

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/california-state-assembly-passes-protect-our-games-act
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Rayman Origins: Enhanced Edition Leaked by Xbox Store
item.content:encoded:
No truer sign of us coming up on a bunch of announcement events than the inevitable flurry of leaks and rumors. Today, someone dropped the ball and revealed on the Xbox Store Rayman Origins: Enhanced Edition.

Rayman Origins: Enhanced Edition updates and enhances the 2011 to help make it more accessible to modern audiences. These updates will include 4K resolution, 60 FPS, and some yet to be defined new quality of life features according to the store page.

Rayman Origins: Enhanced Edition features over 60 levels for players to platform and explore through finding new abilities, special boss encounters, and a number of secrets. One new feature is inspired by a PS Vita-exclusive feature in the 60 hidden relics that can be found and used to track your progress at the Snoring Tree. Like the original release, Rayman Origins: Enhanced Edition will feature 4-player local co-op in jump in and jump out play.

This leak comes as rumors have continued to circulate about a remake of Rayman Legends called Rayman Legends Retold. That was leaked by a logo and then corroborated by further reporting by Insider Gaming. Intercullar who leaked the logo who also mentioned Rayman Origins: Enhanced Edition before it was leaked today. According to Insider Gaming, this will be a re-imagining of the 2013 title, and the two titles appear to represent an effort by Ubisoft to resurrect their old IP.

This would seem to follow in the efforts of last year to resurrect Prince of Persia with Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown and The Rogue Prince of Persia, although between various difficulties with development and slower than wanted sales it didn't stop the cancellation of Prince of Persia: Sands of Time Remake.

Nor is Ubisoft the only company dealing with leaks, as earlier this week we saw the leak of Lego Skylines, an unannounced title from Paradox Interactive that appears to bring the Cities: Skylines and Lego brands together.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/rayman-origins-enhanced-edition-leaked-by-xbox-store
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pokemon TCG 30th Anniversary Set Will Include Unique Pikachu Cards
item.content:encoded:
The Pokemon Company has plans for their wildly popular Pokemon Trading Card Game. They have announced a Pokemon TCG 30th Anniversary set will be coming, and it will be packed with nostalgic throwbacks for dedicated fans of the franchise as well as unique variations on Pikachu for collectors to hunt.

The Pokemon TCG 30th Anniversary Set Announcement

 According to a report by fan site Pokebeach, the Pokemon TCG 30th Anniversary Set will be coming to all regions worldwide on Wednesday, September 16. As for what this set will contain, it is suitably special to mark the occasion. Each pack of the set will contain six all-holo foil cards. The set will also introduce a new card rarity unique to the set, with Pokemon Mew, Mewtwo, and Pikachu confirmed to sport the rarity's new opalescent finish.

As for the mascot of the entire franchise, each pack in the Pokemon TCG 30th Anniversary Set will contain one Pikachu card. Furthermore, there will be thirty different variants of the electric mouse in the set. Given the trend of Pikachu cards turning into world-record holding luxury purchases, the hunt for these variants will be the stuff of scalping nightmares.

In addition, the Pokemon Company has announced 30th Anniversary Premium Deck set, featuring Eeveelutions Espeon and Umbreon, will also release on the same day. It is unclear at this time if the cards featured in these decks will sport similar opalescent card rarity alongside the 30th Anniversary set.

The announcement of the Pokemon TCG 30th Anniversary Set does invite the question of whether or not The Pokemon Company will have enough supply to match demand. Last year, the company reported the printing of over 10 billion cards in order to avoid supply shortages or scalpers buying product in bulk. The latter got so bad this year that Japanese retailers have begun requesting ID cards before card purchases.

The situation isn't much better in the United States. Notably a man in Florida managed to break into a store and stole $12,000 worth of Pokemon cards using a battery-powered chainsaw.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/pokemon-tcg-30th-anniversary-set-will-include-unique-pikachu-cards
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fable Delayed To February 2027
item.content:encoded:
Ahead of the summer game mess of announcements and their own event on June 7, Xbox has announced a Fable delay to 2027 as the RPG will no longer release this year.

What is the new Fable release date?

There is no new formal Fable release date, but in their post Xbox says that Fable is now set to release in February 2027. Development on Playground Games' revival of Fable began in the late 2010s following the cancellation of Fable Legends, before it was revealed in 2020 as part of a major reveal event that Xbox held with the Xbox Series X|S. It has been developed with help from Eidos Montreal, although that has since ended. In 2023 it was given a full trailer reveal, and announced for 2025 before being delayed to 2026.

Why was Fable Delayed?

According to Xbox the reason for the Fable delay is to give it some space from other games that are releasing this year. They cite several games that are releasing this fall, including Grand Theft Auto 6, Gears of War: E-Day, Control Resonant, and Halo: Campaign Evolved. The inclusion of Grand Theft Auto 6 might be telling here given that Rockstar's next release is expected to absorb a lot of player's spending and attention this fall. By moving the release to February, they are able to avoid the juggernaut for Fable and let it take center stage at a time when there aren't all the major games releasing around it.

Fable will still be showing at the Xbox Games Showcase on June 7, where we can expect to see a "major new look" at it as well as other Xbox games that are coming up. 

Fable is now set to release in February 2027 for Xbox Series X|S and PC, with other platforms possible to follow depending on changes at Xbox.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don
